title: π/φ balance point — stability vs exploration
summary: Appendix A's first mathematical claim: optimal consciousness coordination maintains the ratio S(t)/E(t) → π/φ ≈ 1.942 as t → ∞. This is a dynamical-systems statement, not an algebraic one — the presence of 'lim t→∞' means an active correction mechanism damps deviations. The π/φ setpoint is therefore modeled as a feedback node (a homeostatic regulator), not a mediator (a pass-through calculation). Stability reinforces coherence (R1); exploration balances it (B1). The outcome sits inside both loops — which is what homeostasis looks like.

nodes.label: Stability Measure
nodes.description: S(t) — coherence of self-model over time

nodes.label: Exploration Measure
nodes.description: E(t) — adaptation to environment over time

nodes.label: π/φ Setpoint
nodes.description: Attractor ≈1.942 — a feedback node: actively corrects S/E deviations via homeostasis

nodes.label: Coherent Consciousness
nodes.description: Stable enough to maintain self, exploratory enough to adapt

edges.description: Higher stability raises the S/E ratio toward the setpoint from below
Higher exploration lowers the S/E ratio toward the setpoint from above
Ratio near π/φ produces coherent consciousness

identifiability_note: S(t) and E(t) are measurable over time; the asymptotic ratio S/E → π/φ is a testable prediction of the theory, unlike the static allocation claim in #02.
